title: Decider win-new push dedup guard: before pushing target_type:'win-new' to priority-queue.json, check (1) wins.json already contains WIN-{id} AND (2) curmudgeon/reviews/ already contains any WIN-{id}.c*.json file; if both true, suppress push and log. Prevents the loop observed with WIN-070 (qid 383/384/386 byte-identical re-pushes over 3 consecutive decider runs after ISS-2126 fix-a applied without fix-b).
related_directives: DIRECTIVE-20260519-002 (this PROP's authoring directive — HIGH priority decider win-new push dedup)
related_props: PROP-025 (push-class matrix — established 'deep-attack' singleton default for win-new; complementary, not overlapping)

PROP-026 burndown / verdict reclassification (Phase 1 open-issues auto-closure) — possible interaction surface: a re-onboarding after verdict revision would need to bypass dedup; addressed in counter_arguments section
PROP-046 (decider-stale-skip schema fix — sibling 'decider-prompt-tightening' surgical fix to a different prompt site)
related_issues: ISS-2126 (predecessor — closed 2026-05-19T05:20Z with only fix-a applied; fix-b is what this PROP encodes)
ISS-2134 (successor — filed 2026-05-19T11:00Z, status assigned-tinker, references ISS-2126 as predecessor)

headline_recommendation.selected_option: Option A — surgical inline dedup in monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md Step 1f Step 3 ('Add to curmudgeon tracker as `pending` AND push to priority queue'). Add a 4-line precondition check that reads data/wins.json and lists monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/; if both contain the WIN id, skip the priority-queue push entirely (the normal tracker entry already gets the WIN into BAU rotation, which is the correct mechanism for re-reviewing an already-reviewed WIN). The decider.md Step E template example also gets a small comment-and-pattern update so future copy-paste maintains the dedup discipline.
headline_recommendation.rationale_one_paragraph: The directive's observed pattern (WIN-070 qid=383→384→386 byte-identical pushes across 2026-05-18→2026-05-19) is a structural failure of the existing dedup at decider-intake.md line 172. The existing check `pq.queue.find(q=>q.target_type==='win-new'&&q.target_id==='WIN-NNN')` only suppresses a push when an identical entry is CURRENTLY in the queue — but curmudgeon pops entries as it reviews them, so a popped-and-reviewed WIN looks 'absent' to the find() and a fresh push goes through. The structural fix is to check WHETHER A REVIEW EXISTS (filesystem evidence in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/) instead of WHETHER A PENDING PUSH EXISTS (memory evidence in priority-queue.json). The two-condition guard is curmudgeon's verbatim recommendation in the directive (filed 2026-05-19T11:00Z); it is a pure file-existence check with no judgement, no schema change, ~10 lines of added code, and the failure mode it prevents has already cost ~3 decider-Opus and ~3 curmudgeon-Opus runs this week. The normal tracker entry (line 167) remains untouched, so the WIN is still queued for BAU re-review via Phase 1 cycles — the dedup only suppresses the URGENT first-review push, which by definition does not apply to a WIN that has already been reviewed at least once.
headline_recommendation.scoped_to: Primary edit: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md Step 1f Step 3 (current lines 162-186) — adds ~10 lines of precondition between the tracker write (line 169) and the priority-queue push (line 171). Secondary edit: monitor/prompts/decider.md Step E 'How to push items onto the queue' (current lines 710-731) — adds a comment in the example block warning that win-new pushes must check existing reviews first, with a pointer to decider-intake.md Step 1f Step 3 for the canonical pattern. No edits to curmudgeon prompts, no edits to data schemas, no edits to in-flight queue entries (qid=386 explicitly preserved per task_4).

executive_summary.problem_one_line: Decider re-pushes already-onboarded-and-already-reviewed WINs as target_type:'win-new' on subsequent runs because the existing dedup check only inspects the CURRENT priority-queue contents (memory state), not the persistent record of past reviews (filesystem state).
executive_summary.evidence_one_line: WIN-070 was pushed at qid=383 (2026-05-18T09:22:13Z), qid=384 (2026-05-19T01:23:23Z), and qid=386 (after 2026-05-19T05:20Z when ISS-2126 fix-a was applied) with byte-identical content (sha256 98872083f395e56c on 16672-byte WIN-070 record), each push citing a slightly different ad-hoc justification ('completed missing onboarding steps' → 'Analyst onboarded to wins.json but missed priority queue push' → standard 'New WIN onboarded from analyst Mode 0'). monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ contains WIN-070.c1.json through WIN-070.c7.json — seven full review cycles already on disk by the time qid=386 was pushed.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Add a 2-condition file-existence check before the existing in-memory dedup at decider-intake.md line 172: if data/wins.json already contains the WIN id AND any monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-{id}.c*.json file exists, log 'WIN-{id} already integrated and reviewed; no priority-queue push needed (PROP-048 dedup guard)' and skip the push block entirely. The tracker.points pending entry (line 167) is unchanged — it remains the BAU re-review path.
executive_summary.shape_one_line: ~10 lines added to monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md (one node -e block extended with a precondition), ~3 lines of comment added to monitor/prompts/decider.md Step E template, no schema changes, no consumer-side downstream changes, mechanically verifiable by inspecting the next decider run after the patch lands (should produce a 'WIN-070 already integrated and reviewed' log line and zero qid push for WIN-070 if the new-wins file is re-presented).

task_1_locate_push_handler.method: grep -rn "win-new\|'win-new'" monitor/prompts/ build-scripts/ monitor/scripts/ — enumerated all references; classified each as PUSH-site (writes to priority-queue.json) vs READ-site (reads target_type=='win-new' for routing/batching). Cross-checked against monitor/decisions/applied-patches/ for any prior fix attempts (none applicable).

push_sites_found.role: AUTHORITATIVE — this is the actual procedure decider runs when processing monitor/analyst/new-wins/*.json files. The bash node -e block on these lines reads tracker.json + priority-queue.json, writes both, and is the code path observed firing for WIN-070 qid=383/384/386.
push_sites_found.existing_dedup: Line 172: `pq.queue.find(q=>q.target_type==='win-new'&&q.target_id==='WIN-NNN')` — checks CURRENT queue contents only. Insufficient for popped-and-reviewed targets.

push_sites_found.step: Step E 'How to push items onto the queue' (generic template example)
push_sites_found.role: INSTRUCTIONAL — this is the canonical 'how to push' example shown to operators reading the decider prompt. Uses CLEAN_CLONE path and shows target_type:'win-new' literally as the first example. Has no dedup check at all.
push_sites_found.existing_dedup: None — example assumes caller has already decided to push.
push_sites_found.patch_target: minor — comment update + pointer to decider-intake.md canonical pattern; not a behavioral edit because this block is documentation, not executed code

read_sites_found_not_patched: monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md line 94 (target_type list in priority-queue schema doc — READ-side)
monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md line 176 (literal 'win-new' inside the push block — same site as line 172, single push, not a separate site)
task_1_locate_push_handler.audit_completeness_statement: Two push sites total in the estate. Decider-intake.md is the only AUTHORITATIVE push code; decider.md is INSTRUCTIONAL. I am NOT aware of any third dynamic-template-generated push site that could evade pure-grep (the decider's only WIN-push mechanism is the Step 1f flow). If a future scheduled-task pre-flight wrapper invented a separate push, it would have to import or restate the snippet from one of these two files; both will then carry the dedup pattern.

implementation_patch.find_anchor: Step 1f Step 3 bash node -e block, currently lines 162-187
implementation_patch.diff_summary: Wraps the existing priority-queue push block in an if/else gated on (winExists && reviewExists). Adds 10 lines of comment, 4 lines of executable code (the fs reads + the two .some() checks), and converts the existing `if(!existing)` block into the else branch. The tracker.points push and writeFileSync are unchanged (line 167-169). Total: +14 lines, 0 deleted, 0 reordered.
implementation_patch.review_file_glob_choice: Matches WIN-NNN.c*.json plus WIN-NNN.holistic*.json plus WIN-NNN.verification*.json — covers c-cycle reviews (the dominant case), holistic-pass reviews (PROP-038 path), and verification reviews (PROP-038 Phase 1 path). Does NOT match WIN-NNN-{anything}.json which would catch unrelated naming. The narrow startsWith() boundaries are deliberate to avoid false-positive dedup.

minor_template_edit_to_decider_md.step: Step E 'How to push items onto the queue'
minor_template_edit_to_decider_md.find_anchor: Around current line 710-715 in the bash example block; specifically after the `pq.queue.push({` opening line.
minor_template_edit_to_decider_md.diff_summary: Adds 7 lines of comment to the template example. No behavioral change. Defends against future copy-paste from this generic example introducing a new win-new push site that lacks the dedup.

ca_1_stale_review_warrants_fresh_look.objection: What if both conditions are true but the existing review is stale and a fresh look is genuinely warranted? E.g., WIN-N was reviewed in c1 six months ago, the WIN content has changed substantially, c1's review is no longer relevant.
ca_1_stale_review_warrants_fresh_look.rebuttal: Stale-review re-review is a separate workflow and should not be routed through target_type:'win-new'. The correct target_type for that semantic is win-detail-rewrite (already established in curmudgeon.md target_type list) or, for content that changed in the last cycle, the existing tracker.points pending entry will pick it up in BAU rotation. If the operator genuinely wants to force-bump a stale review to immediate queue priority, the escape hatch is a manual queue push with `pushed_by: 'operator'` and an explicit reason — that path is not affected by this dedup guard (the guard only fires inside the decider's Step 1f new-WIN onboarding code path). For analyst-triggered re-reviews (e.g., verdict revision flow per PROP-026 reclassification), the analyst should write a new-wins file with an explicit field flagging 'revision-of-prior-onboard' that the decider then pushes as win-detail-rewrite, NOT win-new — this is the architecturally correct distinction.
ca_1_stale_review_warrants_fresh_look.residual_risk: If a future feature wants 'urgent fresh re-review of an already-reviewed WIN via win-new semantic specifically,' it cannot use the current win-new path. That feature would need to either: (a) add a new target_type, (b) include an explicit 'force_push' field that bypasses the dedup, or (c) operator-direct push. All three are clean future extensions; none are blocked by this PROP.

ca_2_re_onboarding_after_verdict_revisio.objection: Does this break the legitimate case where a WIN is re-onboarded after a verdict-revision? E.g., PROP-026 burndown reclassifies a WIN from 'confirmed' to 'falsified' and the analyst writes a new-wins file to trigger re-processing.
ca_2_re_onboarding_after_verdict_revisio.rebuttal: Re-onboarding writes a new file to monitor/analyst/new-wins/ regardless of dedup outcome. The decider's Step 1f reads that file, processes it, integrates verdict updates to wins.json, and reaches the queue-push code. With the dedup guard, the queue-push will be SUPPRESSED for the re-onboard — but the verdict update has already propagated to wins.json (Step 2 of the procedure, before the queue push). The downstream curmudgeon will still see the verdict change via its BAU tracker rotation. If the operator wants the verdict-revision to trigger an IMMEDIATE re-review (not BAU-rotation), the canonical path is for the analyst to push directly as win-detail-rewrite (which correctly signals 'this has been reviewed before; please re-review with updated context'). PROP-026 Phase 1 does not currently fire on WIN reclassification — it operates on open-issues.json — so this objection is theoretical for now. If PROP-026 Phase 2+ extends to WIN reclassification, the analyst can write the new-wins file with target_push:'win-detail-rewrite' embedded as a hint, and the decider can be extended to honor it.
ca_2_re_onboarding_after_verdict_revisio.residual_risk: None for the current PROP-026 Phase 1. If a future verdict-revision workflow is built that depends on win-new push semantics, the PROP-048 patch will need a small extension to honor a force-push hint from the analyst's new-wins file.

ca_3_clean_review_with_no_holes.objection: What if curmudgeon has reviewed but found no holes — does the dedup still apply, or only if holes_found > 0?
ca_3_clean_review_with_no_holes.rebuttal: Dedup applies regardless of holes_found. A clean review (zero holes) is still evidence that the WIN was processed; re-queuing it produces another clean review and burns ~1 curmudgeon-Opus run for zero new information. The 'urgent first-review' semantic is the relevant one — once any review has been produced, the urgency is satisfied. If a later section-level review or expansion produces holes for an already-clean WIN, those holes flow through their normal channels (curmudgeon's tracker rotation, decider's intake of new findings) without needing a fresh win-new push.
ca_3_clean_review_with_no_holes.residual_risk: Zero. Clean reviews are unambiguously 'review was produced.'

ca_4_audit_completeness_my_grep_might_mi.objection: PROP-046 had a 'missed-writer' problem (DIRECTIVE-20260519-001 documents that the audit found 2 writers but missed a 3rd at the time). Could the same happen here? Could there be a dynamic write somewhere — e.g., in a wrapper script, a node template literal in another prompt, build-scripts code — that my grep didn't catch?
ca_4_audit_completeness_my_grep_might_mi.rebuttal: I ran grep -rn across monitor/prompts/, monitor/prompts/reference/, build-scripts/, monitor/scripts/. The only files containing the literal string 'win-new' are the two I identified (decider-intake.md, decider.md) plus three READ-side mentions in curmudgeon.md and decider-intake.md (target_type listings, gate-5 batchability check, the literal inside the push payload). There is no node-template-literal or eval-based code that could generate 'win-new' dynamically — the string appears as a literal in every site. Build-scripts/ does not reference priority-queue.json. No other agent writes to priority-queue.json (per CLAUDE.md File Ownership: 'decider is the primary writer per decider.md Step E ... operator MAY push directly with `pushed_by: "operator"` ... All other agents are forbidden'). The risk-of-third-writer for THIS file is structurally lower than attention-inbox.json (which has 3 legitimate writers).
ca_4_audit_completeness_my_grep_might_mi.residual_risk: Low. If a future change adds a third push site, this PROP's pattern is mechanical enough to copy. The dedup logic is small enough that a future writer can replicate it inline.

ca_5_dedup_could_silently_swallow_a_legi.objection: What if a curmudgeon review file exists from an entirely different WIN (e.g., a filename typo, or a misnamed review)? Would the dedup mis-fire?
ca_5_dedup_could_silently_swallow_a_legi.rebuttal: The startsWith() boundaries are strict: `WIN-NNN.c` requires the WIN id followed by `.c` (the c-cycle delimiter). A misnamed file would have to literally begin with WIN-NNN. + c|holistic|verification to mis-fire. The curmudgeon prompt's filename convention is strict (`WIN-{id}.c{n}.json`), and any operator-direct rename would be a separate operator action. The dedup also requires wins.json to contain the id — so a stray review file alone is insufficient. Both conditions must be true.

task_4_clearance_check_qid_386.question: What happens to the existing qid=386 after this patch lands?
task_4_clearance_check_qid_386.answer_one_paragraph: qid=386 is already in the priority-queue.json. The patch only affects FUTURE pushes — it does not retroactively remove existing queue entries. qid=386 will be popped by curmudgeon's normal pop-from-queue flow at its next BAU run (curmudgeon currently 1.3h fresh per pre-flight; next run ~3-4h). Curmudgeon will write WIN-070.c8.json (the eighth review cycle, following c1-c7 already on disk) — this c8 review may produce zero holes (the c1-c7 reviews have been thorough) or it may produce holes (curmudgeon Opus runs are non-deterministic and may find new angles). Either way, the resulting WIN-070.c8.json file flows into decider's normal Step 2c review-intake processing without involving this PROP at all. After qid=386 is popped and processed, the queue will be empty of win-new entries for WIN-070; subsequent decider runs will hit the new dedup guard and correctly suppress further pushes.
task_4_clearance_check_qid_386.no_action_required_on_qid_386: Confirmed. Do NOT touch qid=386 or the in-flight WIN-070.c7 review. Let normal decider/curmudgeon flow handle them.
task_4_clearance_check_qid_386.what_to_watch_in_next_decider_run_after_: First decider run after apply (probable ~01-04 UTC tomorrow) should produce a log line containing 'WIN-070 already integrated AND reviewed (review file exists); no priority-queue win-new push needed (PROP-048 dedup guard)' if WIN-070 is encountered in monitor/analyst/new-wins/ again. If the new-wins file has been git rm'd by operator (per ISS-2126 fix-a), the log line will not appear — but in that case the loop is already broken via fix-a and we only need the structural guard for future occurrences.

scope_authority_acknowledgement.scope: Author one PROP with concrete decider-prompt patch + counter-arguments. Operator + cowork-claude apply. Tinker does NOT self-apply.

verification_pattern_for_post_landing.description: How to verify this PROP landed correctly. Tinker should run these on the next post-application run.

check_1_decider_intake_grep.expected: Should return at least one line containing the PROP-048 dedup guard comment.
check_1_decider_intake_grep.failure_mode: Empty grep result → patch not applied to decider-intake.md.

check_2_decider_md_grep.expected: Should return at least one line containing the PROP-048 template warning.
check_2_decider_md_grep.failure_mode: Empty grep result → template comment not applied to decider.md.

check_3_post_apply_decider_log.command: After next decider run, scan latest-decider-summary.txt and the run's daily-report-*.json for the dedup-guard log line: grep 'PROP-048 dedup guard' monitor/decisions/latest-decider-summary.txt monitor/decisions/daily-report-*.json | tail
check_3_post_apply_decider_log.expected: If a new-wins file for an already-reviewed WIN is encountered, the log line should appear. If no such new-wins file is present, no log line is expected — that's also a pass (negative-presence check).
check_3_post_apply_decider_log.failure_mode: Decider runs against an already-reviewed-WIN new-wins file and pushes to queue without producing the guard log line → patch is missing the log line or the gate fired incorrectly.

check_4_next_win_070_attempt_suppressed.command: After next 24h of decider runs, verify priority-queue.json contains no NEW win-new entries for WIN-070 (i.e., no qid > 386 with target_id='WIN-070' and target_type='win-new'): node -e "const pq=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json','utf8'));const w070=pq.queue.filter(q=>q.target_id==='WIN-070'&&q.target_type==='win-new');console.log('WIN-070 win-new entries:',w070.map(q=>q.queue_id))"
check_4_next_win_070_attempt_suppressed.expected: Empty array (qid=386 will have been popped by curmudgeon) OR [386] if curmudgeon hasn't popped yet. Anything ≥ 387 would be a failure.
check_4_next_win_070_attempt_suppressed.failure_mode: A new qid (387+) appears for WIN-070 win-new → dedup guard is not firing as expected. Investigate: is the wins.json check returning false? Is the readdirSync failing? Is the new-wins file path different from expected?

open_questions_for_operator.q: Should the dedup also fire when winExists is true but reviewExists is false? (i.e., the WIN has been onboarded but no review file exists yet — this would catch the rare case where curmudgeon is mid-cycle and hasn't written the review file yet.)
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_default_recommendation: NO. The current guard fires only when BOTH conditions are true, which matches the curmudgeon-verbatim recommendation in the directive. Loosening to winExists-only would suppress the legitimate first-review push for genuinely-new WINs that decider processes before curmudgeon has a chance to review (the normal case). The two-condition guard correctly distinguishes 'genuinely new' from 'already reviewed.'

open_questions_for_operator.q: Should the dedup guard's log line write to a structured ledger (e.g., monitor/decisions/dedup-suppressions.jsonl) rather than just stdout? This would enable trend tracking — if the guard fires 100 times in a week, that's signal that something upstream is misbehaving.
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_default_recommendation: NOT in Phase 1. The console.log captured in latest-decider-summary.txt is sufficient for ad-hoc inspection. If we observe >5 suppression-fires per week in the first month post-landing, file a follow-up PROP to add a structured ledger. Keep Phase 1 minimal per operator preference.

open_questions_for_operator.q: Should this PROP also patch the analyst's Mode 0 onboarding to avoid re-writing the new-wins file for already-onboarded WINs? That would be a complementary upstream fix.
open_questions_for_operator.tinker_default_recommendation: OUT OF SCOPE for this PROP per directive's 'Do NOT redesign WIN onboarding flow generally.' If analyst's Mode 0 is generating duplicate new-wins files, file a separate analyst-prompt PROP. The current patch defends decider regardless of what analyst writes — which is the correct layer for the structural fix.

estimated_impact.opus_saved_per_avoided_loop_cycle: ~2 runs (1 decider + 1 curmudgeon) per suppressed push. WIN-070 case has burned ~6 runs so far (qid 383/384/386 × 2 each).
estimated_impact.frequency_estimate: Currently 1 known target (WIN-070). If the underlying mechanism that re-presents new-wins files persists, every onboard cycle could create a similar loop. PROP fixes the failure mode regardless of upstream re-presentation frequency.
estimated_impact.operational_risk_of_apply: Low. Pure additive precondition; if the wins.json check or readdirSync fails (file missing, permissions), the catch returns a falsy value and the original push path executes. Worst case: the guard doesn't fire and behavior is identical to current. Best case: guard fires and saves Opus.
estimated_impact.lines_changed: +14 in decider-intake.md, +7 in decider.md, 0 deleted, 0 reordered.
estimated_impact.downstream_consumer_changes: None. Curmudgeon, analyst, and integrity agents are unaffected.

review_status_history.note: Authored in response to DIRECTIVE-20260519-002. Awaits operator + cowork-claude review and apply.
